conversationHistory.content: Hello, thank you for coming in today to discuss the Python Backend Developer position at NovaTech Inc. I'm excited to learn more about your experience with Python, Fast API, and PostgreSQL. Can you start by introducing yourself and telling me a little about your background?

hello Thanks for having me, My name is Abdul Wasiq. and I am 22 years olf, currently I am studying at SZABIST Karachi in 4th semester in software Engineering, and I worked in python for more than 2 years.
What's your experience with Python, and how do you think it's used in backend development?
we use python in backend using some libraries or frameworks, I worked in FAST API, and potgreSQL.
Can you explain the difference between a monolithic architecture and a microservices architecture in backend development?
I am listening it first time but I am curious to know about it.
Let's move on to something you are familiar with, what are the advantages of using Python for backend development?
the biggest advantage of using python in backend is simple syntax, python has simple syntax so it is easy to manage data and other things like communicating between frontend and backend.
How do you handle errors and exceptions in Python, especially when working with APIs like Fast API?

good question, so when I started working in python and I was getting errors like 404 etc and because of that error my whole program was crashing so I searched about it and learnt about try and except and also built in functions like response.ok() which means that if response is between 200 to 299 then it is good otherwise throw in exception and print error without crashing the program.
How did you decide to use try-except blocks versus built-in functions like response.ok() in your Fast API projects?
okay so for example when user enters something that is unexpectable during login or sign up or when user user clicks to go on other page but network is really slow.
Can you walk me through how you've used Fast API to handle a specific API request, like user authentication or data retrieval?
yeah I used Fast API to handle a specific request like for example when user logins then if that data exists in data base then give access to data and show on page.
How do you handle validation for user input in your Fast API projects, especially when dealing with sensitive data like passwords or credit card numbers?

so first I will try to save it in postgreSQL, and try to make it in coded language so that even some one access that password then they should not be able to get password and able to login by using that password.
Can you explain how you specifically use PostgreSQL to store and retrieve sensitive data, like passwords, in a way that's secure and follows best practices?
okay so we use keys, and save whole data not in tables but on other and different tables whose keys are being changed after each hour, and only admin can access these keys to access password.
Can you tell me about a time when you had to optimize the performance of a PostgreSQL database, what steps did you take?

I optimize the postgreSQL for example I was always feeling burn out by looking at messy database but I didnt know how to handle them, so I have learnt by myself that we should divide this data for example user A should have different database and when user B logins then he/she has whole different database so that they dont mix with each other and should not be able to access other user even with mistake.

I appreciate your honesty, but I'm a bit concerned that the approach you described might not be the most efficient way to optimize database performance. It's great that you're thinking about data separation, but there are often more effective methods to achieve that goal.

Thank you so much for coming in today and sharing your experiences with me, I really appreciate the time you took to be here. Before we wrap up, do you have any questions for me - about the role, the team, or the company?

yeah I have question, so can you tell me that in 2026 in AI era, I have weakness and that is I dont fully copy the code from AI, and I write code by myself and disadvantage is that I do work but it takes time and any vibe coder cando that work in 1/3 of my time, but I understand every thing deeply before writing code manually, so my question is that what If I work slowly little bit then will it be issue in your company if I get a job in this company?
